<a id="config-version" name="config-version"></a>
### version

Schema version number. Must be `1` ÔÇö the first stable schema.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `version` | `version` | int | Yes | ÔÇö | int value |

**Example:**

```yaml
version: 1
```

---

<a id="config-vars" name="config-vars"></a>
### vars

User-defined template variable dictionary. Referenced as `{var:name}` anywhere templates are resolved.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `vars` | `vars` | map[string]string | No | ÔÇö | map[string]string value |

**Example:**

```yaml
vars:
  org: prplanit
  repo: stagefreight
```

<a id="config-signing_profiles" name="config-signing_profiles"></a>
### signing_profiles

Named trust profiles for signing release artifacts and images. A profile declares a trust CLASS (`requires`: `key` | `oidc` | `kms` | `hardware`) and assurance requirements ÔÇö never a device, vendor, or cosign flag. Targets reference a profile by id via `signing_profile: <id>` (same pattern as `registry:`). With no profile, the implicit `legacy` default signs images when `COSIGN_KEY` resolves; checksum blobs (SHA256SUMS) sign only under an explicit profile.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | `id` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | string value |
| `requires` | `requires` | []string | Yes | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `ref` | `key.ref` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | string value |
| `issuer` | `oidc.issuer` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `identity` | `oidc.identity` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `ref` | `kms.ref` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | string value |
| `ref` | `pkcs11.ref` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | string value |
| `physical_presence` | `physical_presence` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `non_exportable` | `non_exportable` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `transparency_log` | `transparency_log` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `attestation` | `attestation` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `enforce` | `enforce` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `allow_fallback` | `allow_fallback` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |

> `requires` names the trust class only ÔÇö machinery names (yubikey/fido2/vault/aws) are rejected as classes.
> `physical_presence` (value `required`) is valid only for `requires: hardware`; `non_exportable` is valid for `hardware` OR `kms`.
> Hardware transport is deployment wiring: a `hardware` profile may carry `pkcs11: { ref: <name> }`, bound via `SF_PKCS11_<REF>` to a full `pkcs11:` URI, e.g. `SF_PKCS11_RELEASE='pkcs11:slot-id=0;id=%02;object=SIGN%20key?module-path=/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libykcs11.so'` (YubiKey PIV slot 9c = the digital-signature slot, ykcs11 object id 2). With no `pkcs11` ref the hardware class falls back to FIDO2 (cosign `--sk`). The module path / slot / PIN policy live in the env URI, never in the profile.
> KMS/Vault ref binding is deployment wiring: set `SF_KMS_<REF>` to the URI, e.g. `SF_KMS_RELEASE-SIGNING-KEY=hashivault://release` (cosign's hashivault:// takes the key NAME only).
> OIDC/keyless trust domain is deployment wiring too: `SF_SIGSTORE_{DOMAIN,FULCIO,REKOR,ISSUER,TRUSTED_ROOT,IDENTITY_TOKEN}`. Setting FULCIO/REKOR/TRUSTED_ROOT points cosign at a self-hosted Sigstore (public Fulcio won't trust a self-hosted issuer); ISSUER falls back to the profile's `oidc.issuer`; IDENTITY_TOKEN (value or path) supplies the OIDC token for unattended/non-CI signing (ambient providers used when unset). Standing up Fulcio/Rekor is operator infrastructure, not StageFreight.
> `enforce: true` makes a signing failure fatal; the default is best-effort (recorded as a failed outcome, the build proceeds).
> Aliases normalized at load: `keyless` ÔåÆ `oidc`, `yubikey` ÔåÆ `hardware`.

**Example:**

```yaml
signing_profiles:
  - id: release-key
    requires: key
    key: { ref: "env:COSIGN_KEY" }      # path or env:VAR
  - id: maintainer
    requires: hardware                  # non-exportable key in a signing device
    pkcs11: { ref: release }            # PIV/HSM transport, bound via SF_PKCS11_RELEASE (absent ÔåÆ FIDO2 --sk)
    physical_presence: required
    non_exportable: required
  - id: org-kms
    requires: kms
    kms: { ref: release-signing-key }   # logical ref, bound to a URI at render time
    non_exportable: required            # a KMS/Vault-transit key never leaves the service
```

---

<a id="config-signing" name="config-signing"></a>
### signing

Operational signing configuration (distinct from `signing_profiles`). Governs whether StageFreight may sign, whether it may create/manage a Tier-0 software identity on your behalf, and where that identity persists. `enabled` and `auto_provision` are deliberately separate ÔÇö "signing is encouraged" and "the system minted an identity for me" are not the same thing.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `enabled` | `enabled` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `auto_provision` | `auto_provision` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `type` | `state_dir.type` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `name` | `state_dir.name` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `path` | `state_dir.path` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |

> `enabled: false` disables ALL signing regardless of profiles/keys.
> `auto_provision: true` requires a `state_dir` ÔÇö with no durable storage an ephemeral key would break trust continuity every run.
> With `auto_provision: false` (default), StageFreight never creates key material ÔÇö it signs only with an explicit COSIGN_KEY/profile. Opinionated always-on belongs in a runner/distribution config, not core.
> The Tier-0 identity is created once and NEVER silently regenerated: drift, partial state, or an orphan key is fatal.
> The state_dir must live outside the repository (a key there could be committed, baked into an image, or published).

**Example:**

```yaml
signing:
  enabled: true            # global kill switch (default true)
  auto_provision: false    # explicit consent to create/manage a Tier-0 key (default false)
  state_dir:
    type: volume           # volume | host_path
    name: stagefreight-signing
    # type: host_path
    # path: /srv/stagefreight/signing
```

---

<a id="config-builds" name="config-builds"></a>
### builds

Named build artifacts. Each build has a unique ID referenced by targets. Currently supports `kind: docker`.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | `id` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Unique identifier for this build, referenced by targets. |
| `kind` | `kind` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Build type. Determines which fields are valid. |
| `select_tags` | `select_tags` | []string | No | ÔÇö | Tags for CLI filtering via `--select`. |
| `required` | `required` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `build_mode` | `build_mode` | string | No | (standard) | Build execution strategy. |
| `depends_on` | `depends_on` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `dockerfile` | `dockerfile` | string | No | auto-detected | Path to the Dockerfile. |
| `context` | `context` | string | No | "." | Docker build context path. |
| `target` | `target` | string | No | ÔÇö | Multi-stage build `--target` stage name. |
| `platforms` | `platforms` | []string | No | current OS/arch | Target platforms for multi-arch builds. |
| `build_args` | `build_args` | map[string]string | No | ÔÇö | Key-value pairs passed as `--build-arg`. Supports template variables. |
| `paths` | `cache.watch.paths` | []string | Yes | ÔÇö | Glob patterns for files to watch for changes. |
| `invalidates` | `cache.watch.invalidates` | []string | Yes | ÔÇö | Build stage names invalidated when watched files change. |
| `auto_detect` | `cache.auto_detect` | bool | Yes | true | Auto-detect cache-relevant files from Dockerfile COPY/ADD instructions. |
| `from` | `stage.from` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | string value |
| `as` | `stage.as` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | string value |
| `builder` | `builder` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `command` | `command` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `from` | `from` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `output` | `output` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `image` | `image` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `args` | `args` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `env` | `env` | map[string]string | No | ÔÇö | map[string]string value |
| `compress` | `compress` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `toolchain_image` | `crucible.toolchain_image` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |

**`kind` allowed values:** `docker`

**`build_mode` allowed values:** `(standard)`, `crucible`

> Crucible mode performs a self-proving rebuild to verify build reproducibility.

> Build IDs must be unique across all builds.
> Targets reference builds by name via the `build:` field.

**Example:**

```yaml
builds:
  - id: myapp
    kind: docker
    platforms: [linux/amd64, linux/arm64]
```

---

<a id="config-targets" name="config-targets"></a>
### targets

Distribution targets and side-effects. Each target has a `kind` that determines its behavior: push images, sync READMEs, publish components, or create releases.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | `id` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Unique identifier for this target (logging, status, enable/disable). |
| `kind` | `kind` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Target type. Determines which fields are valid. |
| `build` | `build` | string | No | ÔÇö | References a `builds[].id`. Required for `kind: registry`. |
| `branches` | `when.branches` | []string | No | ÔÇö | Branch filters. Each entry is a policy name or inline regex. |
| `git_tags` | `when.git_tags` | []string | No | ÔÇö | Git tag filters. Each entry is a policy name or inline regex. |
| `events` | `when.events` | []string | No | ÔÇö | CI event type filters. |
| `forges` | `when.forges` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `allow` | `run_from.allow` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `mismatch` | `run_from.mismatch` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `select_tags` | `select_tags` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `registry` | `registry` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `signing_profile` | `signing_profile` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `url` | `url` | string | No | ÔÇö | Registry or forge hostname. |
| `provider` | `provider` | string | No | ÔÇö | Vendor type for auth and API behavior. Auto-detected from URL if omitted on registry/docker-readme targets. |
| `path` | `path` | string | No | ÔÇö | Image path within the registry. |
| `credentials` | `credentials` | string | No | ÔÇö | Env var prefix for authentication. Resolution: try `{PREFIX}_TOKEN` first, else `{PREFIX}_USER` + `{PREFIX}_PASS`. |
| `description` | `description` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `keep_last` | `retention.keep_last` | int | Yes | ÔÇö | Keep the N most recent tags/releases. |
| `keep_daily` | `retention.keep_daily` | int | Yes | ÔÇö | Keep one per day for the last N days. |
| `keep_weekly` | `retention.keep_weekly` | int | Yes | ÔÇö | Keep one per week for the last N weeks. |
| `keep_monthly` | `retention.keep_monthly` | int | Yes | ÔÇö | Keep one per month for the last N months. |
| `keep_yearly` | `retention.keep_yearly` | int | Yes | ÔÇö | Keep one per year for the last N years. |
| `protect` | `retention.protect` | []string | Yes | ÔÇö | Tag patterns that are never deleted. |
| `tags` | `tags` | []string | No | ÔÇö | Tag templates resolved against version info. `kind: registry` only. |
| `native_scan` | `native_scan` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `file` | `file` | string | No | ÔÇö | Path to the README file. `kind: docker-readme` only. |
| `link_base` | `link_base` | string | No | ÔÇö | Base URL for relative link rewriting. `kind: docker-readme` only. |
| `spec_files` | `spec_files` | []string | No | ÔÇö | Component spec file paths. `kind: gitlab-component` only. |
| `catalog` | `catalog` | bool | No | ÔÇö | Enable GitLab Catalog registration. `kind: gitlab-component` only. |
| `aliases` | `aliases` | []string | No | ÔÇö | Rolling git tag aliases. `kind: release` only. |
| `tag` | `tag` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `prerelease` | `prerelease` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `project_id` | `project_id` | string | No | ÔÇö | Project identifier (`owner/repo` or numeric ID). `kind: release`, remote targets only. |
| `mirror` | `mirror` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `sync_release` | `sync_release` | bool | No | ÔÇö | Sync release notes + tags to a remote forge. `kind: release`, remote targets only. |
| `sync_assets` | `sync_assets` | bool | No | ÔÇö | Sync scan artifacts to a remote forge. `kind: release`, remote targets only. |
| `archives` | `archives` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `binary_name` | `binary_name` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `format` | `format` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `name` | `name` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `include` | `include` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `checksums` | `checksums` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `repo` | `repo` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `package` | `package` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `version` | `version` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `project` | `project` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `dir` | `dir` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `exclude` | `exclude` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `domain` | `domain` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `base_path` | `base_path` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `mode` | `versioning.mode` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |

**`kind` allowed values:** `registry`, `docker-readme`, `gitlab-component`, `release`

**`when.events` allowed values:** `push`, `tag`, `release`, `schedule`, `manual`, `pull_request`, `merge_request`

**`provider` allowed values:** `docker`, `ghcr`, `gitlab`, `jfrog`, `harbor`, `quay`, `gitea`, `forgejo`, `ecr`, `gar`, `acr`, `nexus`, `generic`, `github`

> Target IDs must be unique across all targets.
> The `when` block controls routing: all non-empty fields must match (AND logic).

**Example:**

```yaml
targets:
  - id: dockerhub-stable
    kind: registry
    build: myapp
    url: docker.io
    path: myorg/myapp
    tags: ["{version}", "latest"]
    when: { git_tags: [stable], events: [tag] }
    credentials: DOCKER
```

<a id="config-narrator" name="config-narrator"></a>
### narrator

Content composition for file targets. Composes badges, shields, text, includes, and components into managed `<!-- sf:markers -->` sections in any file.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `file` | `file` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Path to the target file. |
| `link_base` | `link_base` | string | No | ÔÇö | Base URL for relative link rewriting. `raw_base` is auto-derived from this. |
| `id` | `items.id` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Item identifier, unique within file. |
| `kind` | `items.kind` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Item type. Determines which fields are valid. |
| `between` | `items.placement.between` | array | No | ÔÇö | Two-element array: `[start_marker, end_marker]`. Content is placed between these markers. |
| `after` | `items.placement.after` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `before` | `items.placement.before` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `heading` | `items.placement.heading` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `mode` | `items.placement.mode` | string | No | replace | How content is placed relative to markers. |
| `inline` | `items.placement.inline` | bool | No | false | Render items side-by-side (space-joined) when true. Default: block (newline-joined). |
| `text` | `items.text` | string | No | ÔÇö | Badge label (left side text). `kind: badge` only. |
| `value` | `items.value` | string | No | ÔÇö | Badge value (right side text, supports templates). `kind: badge` only. |
| `color` | `items.color` | string | No | ÔÇö | Badge color as hex or `auto` (status-driven). `kind: badge` only. |
| `font` | `items.font` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `font_size` | `items.font_size` | int | No | ÔÇö | int value |
| `output` | `items.output` | string | No | ÔÇö | SVG output path for badge generation. `kind: badge` only. |
| `link` | `items.link` | string | No | ÔÇö | Clickable URL. `kind: badge` and `kind: shield`. |
| `shield` | `items.shield` | string | No | ÔÇö | Shields.io path (appended to `https://img.shields.io/`). `kind: shield` only. |
| `content` | `items.content` | string | No | ÔÇö | Raw text/markdown content. Supports template variables. `kind: text` only. |
| `spec` | `items.spec` | string | No | ÔÇö | Component spec file path. `kind: component` only. |
| `path` | `items.path` | string | No | ÔÇö | File path to include verbatim. `kind: include` only. |
| `build` | `items.build` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `source` | `items.source` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `section` | `items.section` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `renderer` | `items.renderer` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `columns` | `items.columns` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `output_file` | `items.output_file` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `wrap` | `items.wrap` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `summary` | `items.summary` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `type` | `items.type` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `params` | `items.params` | map[string]string | No | ÔÇö | map[string]string value |
| `label` | `items.label` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `style` | `items.style` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `logo` | `items.logo` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `catalog` | `items.catalog` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `ref` | `items.ref` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |

**`items.kind` allowed values:** `badge`, `shield`, `text`, `component`, `break`, `include`

**`items.placement.mode` allowed values:** `replace`, `append`, `prepend`, `above`, `below`

**Example:**

```yaml
narrator:
  - file: "README.md"
    link_base: "https://github.com/myorg/myrepo/blob/main"
    items:
      - id: badge.release
        kind: badge
        placement:
          between: ["<!-- sf:badges:start -->", "<!-- sf:badges:end -->"]
          mode: replace
          inline: true
        text: release
        output: ".stagefreight/badges/release.svg"
```

---

<a id="config-lint" name="config-lint"></a>
### lint

Linting configuration. Controls scan mode, module toggles, and per-module options. 9 modules: tabs, secrets, conflicts, filesize, linecount, unicode, yaml, lineendings, freshness.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `preset` | `preset` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `level` | `level` | string | Yes | changed | Scan mode. `changed` scans only modified files; `full` scans everything. |
| `cache_dir` | `cache_dir` | string | Yes | $XDG_CACHE_HOME/stagefreight/<repo-hash>/lint | Override cache directory. |
| `target_branch` | `target_branch` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Target branch for diff-based scanning. |
| `exclude` | `exclude` | []string | Yes | ÔÇö | Glob patterns to exclude from lint scanning. |
| `modules` | `modules` | map[string]object | Yes | ÔÇö | Per-module configuration. Keys: tabs, secrets, conflicts, filesize, linecount, unicode, yaml, lineendings, freshness. |
| `generated` | `provenance.generated` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `vendored` | `provenance.vendored` | []string | No | ÔÇö | []string value |
| `trailing_whitespace` | `remediation.trailing_whitespace` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `final_newline` | `remediation.final_newline` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `max_age` | `cache.max_age` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `max_size` | `cache.max_size` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `fail_on` | `fail_on` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |

**`level` allowed values:** `changed`, `full`

**Example:**

```yaml
lint:
  level: changed
  modules:
    secrets:
      enabled: true
    freshness:
      enabled: true
      options:
        cache_ttl: 300
```

---

<a id="config-security" name="config-security"></a>
### security

Security scanning configuration. Controls vulnerability scanning (Trivy, Grype), SBOM generation (Syft), and how security info appears in release notes.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `preset` | `preset` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `enabled` | `enabled` | bool | Yes | true | Run vulnerability scanning. |
| `required` | `required` | bool | No | ÔÇö | bool value |
| `trivy` | `scanners.trivy` | bool | No | true | Run Trivy image scan. |
| `grype` | `scanners.grype` | bool | No | true | Run Grype image scan. |
| `sbom` | `sbom` | bool | Yes | true | Generate SBOM artifacts via Syft. |
| `fail_on_critical` | `fail_on_critical` | bool | Yes | false | Fail the pipeline if critical vulnerabilities are found. |
| `output` | `output` | string | Yes | .stagefreight/security | Directory for scan artifacts (JSON, SARIF, SBOM, summary). |
| `fail_on` | `fail_on` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `unreachable_vulns` | `unreachable_vulns` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `release_detail` | `release_detail` | string | Yes | counts | Default detail level for security info in release notes. |
| `tag` | `release_detail_rules.tag` | string | No | ÔÇö | Git tag pattern to match. Prefix with `!` to negate. |
| `branch` | `release_detail_rules.branch` | string | No | ÔÇö | Branch pattern to match. Prefix with `!` to negate. |
| `detail` | `release_detail_rules.detail` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Detail level when this rule matches. |
| `max_size` | `cache.trivy.max_size` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `max_age` | `cache.trivy.max_age` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `max_size` | `cache.grype.max_size` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `max_age` | `cache.grype.max_age` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `overwhelm_message` | `overwhelm_message` | []string | Yes | ["ÔÇªmaybe start here:"] | Message lines shown when >1000 vulnerabilities are found. |
| `overwhelm_link` | `overwhelm_link` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | URL appended after overwhelm message. Empty string disables. |

**`release_detail` allowed values:** `none`, `counts`, `detailed`, `full`

**`release_detail_rules.detail` allowed values:** `none`, `counts`, `detailed`, `full`

**Example:**

```yaml
security:
  enabled: true
  scanners:
    trivy: true
    grype: true
  sbom: true
  release_detail: counts
```

---

<a id="config-commit" name="config-commit"></a>
### commit

Commit subsystem configuration. Controls conventional commit formatting, type registry, and default behavior for `stagefreight commit`.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `preset` | `preset` | string | No | ÔÇö | string value |
| `default_type` | `default_type` | string | No | chore | Default commit type when --type is omitted. |
| `default_scope` | `default_scope` | string | No | ÔÇö | Default commit scope when --scope is omitted. |
| `skip_ci` | `skip_ci` | bool | No | false | Append `[skip ci]` to commit subjects by default. |
| `push` | `push` | bool | No | false | Push after committing by default. |
| `conventional` | `conventional` | bool | Yes | true | Use conventional commit format (`type(scope): summary`). |
| `backend` | `backend` | string | No | git | Commit execution backend. |
| `key` | `types.key` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Type identifier used in `--type` flag. Must match `^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$`. |
| `label` | `types.label` | string | Yes | ÔÇö | Human-readable label for documentation and error messages. |
| `alias_for` | `types.alias_for` | string | No | ÔÇö | Resolve this type to another type key. No alias chains allowed. |
| `force_bang` | `types.force_bang` | bool | No | false | Force breaking change indicator (`!`) when this type is used. |

**`backend` allowed values:** `git`, `dry-run`

**Example:**

```yaml
commit:
  default_type: docs
  conventional: true
  backend: git
  skip_ci: false
  push: false
  types:
    - key: feat
      label: Feature
    - key: breaking
      label: Breaking
      force_bang: true
```

---

<a id="config-lifecycle" name="config-lifecycle"></a>
### lifecycle

Selects the repository lifecycle mode ÔÇö the phase graph the pipeline runs. The single most architecturally significant config choice: it determines whether the repo builds container images, validates GitOps manifests, or reconciles governance. When omitted, the lifecycle defaults to `image`.

| Name | YAML Key | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `preset` | `preset` | string | No | ÔÇö | Path to an external lifecycle config fragment to inherit. Resolved and deep-merged before parse; local sibling keys override the preset. Part of the general `preset:` fragment-include mechanism. |
| `mode` | `mode` | string | Yes | image | Selects the lifecycle ÔÇö which phase graph the pipeline runs. Empty defaults to `image`. |

**`mode` allowed values:** `image`, `docker`, `gitops`, `governance`

> `image` (default): the full build ÔåÆ review ÔåÆ publish image pipeline. The only mode where review and publish do work; the others mark them not_applicable.
> `docker`: read-only Docker plan/reconcile (dry-run). Requires the Reconcile capability.
> `gitops`: validate + reconcile Flux manifests ÔÇö audition validates, perform reconciles. Requires Reconcile + ImpactAnalysis (and ClusterAuth when `gitops.cluster` is set).
> `governance`: governance control-repo reconcile ÔÇö distributes config/doctrine to member repos.

> Phase applicability is mode-derived: the `review` and `publish` phases do work only in `image` mode; `gitops`, `governance`, and `docker` mark them not_applicable.
> Capability requirements differ per mode (e.g. `gitops` requires Reconcile + ImpactAnalysis, plus ClusterAuth when `gitops.cluster` is set); the lifecycle backend is checked against them at plan time.

**Example:**

```yaml
lifecycle:
  mode: image
```

---

| `stagefreight` | `clusters.stagefreight` | map[string]any | Yes | ÔÇö | map[string]any value |
